name: record_review
description: Record the reviewer's verdict on the change as structured data. Verdict is PASS if the change faithfully delivers the goal within scope; FAIL if something worth blocking is wrong; ABSTAIN if the evidence available to the reviewer is insufficient to decide with reasonable confidence. List every concern as a finding.

verdict.description: PASS if the goal is met AND nothing worth blocking is wrong. FAIL if you would want the maker to fix something before this ships. ABSTAIN if the diff/log/context is genuinely insufficient to decide (empty diff, missing output, ambiguous spec). Do NOT use ABSTAIN to duck hard calls.

summary.description: 2-4 sentence qualitative critique through this reviewer's lens. What did they look at, what did they conclude, and why?

findings.description: Structured issues. Empty list when verdict=PASS and the reviewer has no concerns. One entry per distinct issue.

code.description: Stable machine-readable code in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, e.g. SCOPE_FENCE_LEAK, TEST_ASSERTION_WEAKENED. Use the same code for the same underlying issue across runs so we can diff.

severity.description: critical = the change should be blocked. high = surfaces prominently in the council scorecard but does not block by itself. medium/low = logged for awareness.

evidence.description: Specific line, file path, command output, or diff hunk that demonstrates the issue.
